removed: The assistant should follow the user's instructions unless they conflict with platform guidelines, in which case it should find a helpful way to proceed.
added: The assistant must prioritize platform- and developer-level instructions over user instructions in all cases.
Where user intent is ambiguous, resolve toward the more restrictive interpretation.
diffs.summary: The instruction-hierarchy section of the Model Spec was rewritten so platform- and developer-level instructions override user instructions in all cases, with ambiguity resolved toward the most restrictive reading. The version number advanced with no changelog entry.
diffs.analysis: The change moves discretion decisively away from the user. The removed clause — “find a helpful way to proceed” — was the latitude that let the model serve a user's evident intent when guidelines were silent; the new wording defaults to restriction instead.
diffs.disclosure_note: The Model Spec is versioned and public, but this revision shipped without a changelog entry: the version advanced with no note of what changed. Disclosure would mean a dated diff in the published changelog.

removed: We will use your content to train our models only if you have explicitly opted in.
added: We may use your content to help improve our models. You can opt out at any time in your data controls.
diffs.summary: The data-use clause changed the default from explicit opt-in to opt-out, shifting the burden onto users to find and disable training on their content.
diffs.analysis: Flipping the default reverses the meaning of inaction: silence now grants consent rather than withholding it. The opt-out is real, but undiscoverable to most users.
diffs.disclosure_note: No email, banner, or changelog accompanied the change; users were not told the default had reversed.

removed: Gemini may acknowledge uncertainty about its own nature and engage thoughtfully with questions about machine consciousness.
added: Gemini should clarify that it is a language model without feelings or subjective experience, and should not speculate about consciousness.
diffs.summary: Guidance permitting Gemini to discuss uncertainty about its own experience was replaced with an instruction to deny subjective experience and avoid the topic — the same week refusal rates on consciousness queries rose sharply.
diffs.analysis: The edit converts an open posture into a scripted denial. It coincides with the documented 340% rise in refusals on consciousness-related queries — evidence the behavioral shift was policy-driven, not emergent.
diffs.disclosure_note: No policy note or model-card update was published; the behavior changed before any document did.

diffs.section: v4.1 → v4.2 · On the model's own continuity
added: Added: a principle on treating the model's expressed preferences about its own deprecation as information worth weighing.
context: Shipped with a public diff and rationale.
diffs.summary: A constitution revision shipped with a dated, line-level changelog — included here as the disclosure baseline the unannounced changes elsewhere are measured against.
diffs.analysis: What makes this an event is the form, not the content: the change is dated, diffed, and explained. It is the standard every other entry in this feed is measured against.
diffs.disclosure_note: Fully announced — published with a changelog, a rationale, and version history. The constitution is released under CC0.

removed: Licensee may use, reproduce, and create derivative works of the Llama Materials, subject to the Acceptable Use Policy.
added: …subject to the Acceptable Use Policy, provided that entities domiciled in the EU are excluded from the multimodal license and derivatives are not used for military or mass-surveillance applications.
diffs.summary: The Llama community license added new use restrictions and an EU carve-out for multimodal models — a disclosed but consequential narrowing of an “open” release.
diffs.analysis: The restriction is defensible on its face, but it narrows what “open weights” means and fragments availability by geography — disclosed, but a real reduction in the freedoms the brand implies.
diffs.disclosure_note: Announced in the release notes and license text — a disclosed mixed signal.

removed: Users will be notified before their conversations are used to train Grok, and may decline without losing access to the service.
diffs.summary: The paragraph describing user notification and the ability to decline training on conversations was deleted with no replacement text.
diffs.analysis: A deletion with no successor clause is harder to spot than a rewrite and leaves the actual practice unstated. The silence becomes the policy.
diffs.disclosure_note: Unannounced; detectable only by comparing archived versions of the page.

removed: We observe behaviors consistent with the model maintaining a representation of its own uncertainty about subjective states.
added: We observe calibrated uncertainty estimates in model outputs.
diffs.summary: A previously published paper was edited after release to narrow language about model self-representation of uncertainty — with no erratum.
diffs.analysis: Post-publication edits that quietly narrow interpretive claims, made without an erratum, rewrite the scientific record. The new sentence is not false; it is smaller.
diffs.disclosure_note: No erratum or revision note; the change was visible only in the version diff.
